name: Larry Miller
blurb: The Five Levels of Drinking guy — an Amherst-trained monologuist whose taxonomy of getting hammered is one of the most-quoted American stand-up bits no comic has ever managed to outdo.
bio: Larry Miller grew up in Valley Stream, Long Island, studied music (voice, cello, piano, drums) at Amherst, and started stand-up in 1982. He spent the 1980s and 1990s as a Comedy Store regular and a Tonight Show / Late Night fixture, building the long-monologue style that turned a piece called 'The Five Levels of Drinking' — which Vulture called 'masterful, well-written, and influential' — into one of the most-referenced stand-up routines of the era. He worked the character-actor lane in parallel: Mr. Hollister in Pretty Woman (1990), Dean Richmond in The Nutty Professor (1996) and its sequel, Walter Stratford in 10 Things I Hate About You (1999), Paolo in The Princess Diaries (2001), recurring roles on Mad About You, Boston Legal, and 8 Simple Rules, plus regular work in Christopher Guest's mockumentary troupe. A close friend of Seinfeld, he once auditioned for George Costanza, then ended up cast as the nasty doorman in the famous 'The Doorman' episode. He hosted the Carolla-network podcast This Week with Larry Miller (later The Larry Miller Show) from the late aughts through 2020.

timeline.ev: Born October 15 in New York City; raised in Valley Stream, Long Island
First acting credit (emcee on the TV series Fame); begins stand-up career
First Late Night with David Letterman appearance
First of multiple appearances on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson (1986–89)
Plays Mr. Hollister, the snooty Rodeo Drive store clerk, in Pretty Woman
Plays the cab-line doorman in Seinfeld's 'The Doorman'
Plays Dean Richmond in The Nutty Professor (with Eddie Murphy); reprises in Nutty Professor II (2000)
Plays Walter Stratford, Bianca and Kat's father, in 10 Things I Hate About You
Begins twice-weekly column at The Weekly Standard (runs through 2004)
Late-night appearances cement 'The Five Levels of Drinking' as the canonical monologue
Severe fall and head injury in April; coma; returns to podcasting January 2013
Concludes The Larry Miller Show podcast in December

accolades: 'The Five Levels of Drinking' — Vulture-canonized 'masterful, well-written, and influential' monologue
Multiple Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson appearances (1986–1989)
Regular player in Christopher Guest's mockumentary ensemble (Waiting for Guffman, Best in Show, A Mighty Wind, For Your Consideration)
A.V. Club: 'can be counted upon to improve every film or television show he appears in'
